topPRs.title: test(ui): fix neutered story/aesthetic gates + add missing chat-widget coverage (#9304)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## Summary

Strengthens UI test/validation/verification per #9304, on top of the chat-widget work already on `develop`. The headline is **fixing the gates that were silently passing while testing nothing**, plus filling genuinely-missing coverage. Branch cut clean off latest `develop`; duplicates already on develop (the `WIDGET_MATRIX.md`, the `use-inline-widget-context` hook, the choice/edge/streaming parser tests) are intentionally **not** re-added. Full 13-dimension audit (134 findings) is in #9304.

## Broken/neutered gates this fixes (all still broken on develop)

- **Storybook story gate was testing NOTHING.** `run-story-gate.mjs` read `document.documentElement.classList` for `sb-show-*`, but **Storybook 10 applies those to `document.body`** (verified vs installed `storybook@10.4.x`). All ~1495 stories were misclassified `needs-runtime`; the blank/console/a11y/story-threw checks were dead. Fixed to check `body` (+`documentElement` fallback); app-context throws now classify soft `needs-runtime`; added a self-check that hard-fails if the whole catalog comes back `needs-runtime`. This surfaced + I fixed **5 real hidden bugs** (AppsView `new Set(favoriteApps)` — the `createMockApp()` Proxy returned a `noop` function for absent props; 2 shell story-arg bugs). Populated the empty `console`/`a11y` baselines (enforcement was OFF) + added a `broken-baseline` ratchet. Result on develop's catalog: **good=1413, broken=0**.
- **Aesthetic audit missed the brand accent.** `bucket()` was `g>90` and bucketed the shipped `--accent-rgb: 255,88,0` as `neutral`, so the no-blue/orange-hover detector skipped the real brand button. Replaced with **hue-based bucketing**. 21/21 rules tests incl. the `255,88,0` regression.
- **Aesthetic audit never gated** + ran in **no workflow.** Added `ELIZA_AUDIT_APP_STRICT` strict mode + shrinking debt allowlist + new `app-aesthetic-audit.yml` CI.
- **Matrix gate was a weak `arrayContaining` smoke** → exact-set + `inline-registry.matrix.test.tsx`.

## New coverage (43 assertions, verified green against develop)

`parser-parity.contract.test.ts` (UI parsers vs `@elizaos/core` `findInteractionRegions` agree on CHOICE/FOLLOWUPS; FORM divergence pinned); `followups.behavior.test.tsx` (had **zero** behavior tests); `MessageContent.config.test.tsx` (`[CONFIG]`/`InlinePluginConfig` had **zero** tests); messages/notifications populated-state; `AppsView.mockapp.test.tsx`; stories for the 6 story-less widgets. Wired `task-widget-in-chat` e2e into the keyless `scenario-pr.yml` lane (ratchet 3→2) + wired 2 unclassified perf specs failing the coverage gate.

## Followups (documented, intentionally not rushed here)

The overlay→`MessageContent` content unification ([CONFIG]/code on the dark overlay bubble) + the `rate_limited`/`provider_issue` Retry need a dark-surface `MessageContent` variant + the `audit:app` visual loop (documented in `WIDGET_MATRIX.md`); `[form:submit]` has no server consumer; the determinism gate's interprocedural enhancement + dead-code removal need careful rollout. These are primary-surface/large changes deferred to a focused follow-up rather than shipped unverified.

## Evidence

Story gate proven `broken=0` on develop's catalog (60/60 Primitives `good`, was 0); 43 new assertions pass; the now-functional gate produces the screenshot/contact-sheet artifacts.

topPRs.title: Feat/plugin trust layer for AI Agents
topPRs.author: stvor-hq
topPRs.body:
# Relates to

New plugin. Opens discussion around ERC-8183 as a lifecycle standard for agent-to-agent commerce in elizaOS.

# Sync with develop

- [x] Rebased onto latest `origin/develop`
- [x] `bun run verify` passes

Within `packages/plugin-agent-commerce-pqc`:

$ bun run verify

✓ TypeScript type-check passes
✓ Vitest suite passes

35 tests passed.

# Risks

Medium.

This PR adds a new opt-in plugin under `packages/plugin-agent-commerce-pqc`. No existing runtime behavior is modified.

The plugin registers:

- 1 Service (`agent_commerce`)
- 4 Actions
- 1 Provider

State is persisted through the existing `runtime.createMemory()` API using the `erc8183:job:` namespace.

Known operational risks:

- hydration currently loads up to 1000 job records per agent
- rate limiting is process-local and not distributed
- reputation persistence is memory-backed by default

# Background

## What does this PR do?

Adds an ERC-8183-inspired commerce workflow for elizaOS agents.

The plugin enables one agent to:

1. Create a job
2. Fund a job
3. Submit a deliverable
4. Evaluate the result
5. Complete, refund, expire, or abort the workflow

State machine:

OPEN → FUNDED → SUBMITTED → COMPLETE
                           ↘ REFUND
              ↘ EXPIRED
         ↘ ABORTED

## Registered extension points

| Type | Name |
|--------|--------|
| Service | agent_commerce |
| Action | CREATE_SECURE_JOB |
| Action | FUND_SECURE_JOB |
| Action | SUBMIT_DELIVERABLE |
| Action | JOB_STATUS |
| Provider | COMMERCE_CONTEXT |

## Persistence

Jobs are stored through `runtime.createMemory()` using:

erc8183:job:<jobId>

Each state transition appends a new memory record.

On restart, the store lazily hydrates from memory records and reconstructs the latest job state using the highest `updatedAt` value.

## Security features

- SHA-256 payload attestation
- Deliverable hash verification
- Reputation-based funding gate
- Rate limiting
- State transition validation
- Idempotent terminal transitions

## What kind of change is this?

Feature (non-breaking).

All changes are additive.

# Documentation changes needed?

No.

The package includes its own README.

# Testing

## Where should a reviewer start?

- `src/elizaos/index.ts`
- `src/service.ts`
- `src/state-machine.ts`
- `src/store/elizaos.ts`

## Detailed testing steps

Run:

```bash
cd packages/plugin-agent-commerce-pqc

bun run verify

Expected result:

35 tests passed
0 failures

Optional lifecycle demo:

LOG_LEVEL=info bun run demo-lifecycle.ts

The demo exercises:

- createJob
- fundJob
- submitJob
- evaluateJob
- abort path
- persistence path

Evidence
Real LLM-call trajectory
N/A.

The plugin currently exposes deterministic actions and state-machine logic. No scenario-runner integration exists yet.

Backend + frontend logs

Verification output:

RUN  v4.1.5

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       35 passed (35)

Exit code: 0

Lifecycle demo output includes:

Created job -> OPEN
Funded job -> FUNDED
Submitted deliverable -> SUBMITTED
Evaluated job -> COMPLETE
Hash mismatch -> ABORTED

Screenshots (before / after) + video walkthrough
N/A — backend plugin, no UI.

Audio / voice walkthrough
N/A.

Known limitations
Hydration loads at most 1000 memory records per agent.
Rate limiting is process-local.
MemoryReputationProvider is not persistent across restarts.
Distributed deployments require a custom reputation backend and shared rate-limit implementation.

topPRs.title: feat(vision): Android on-device screen capture → local vision describe (#9105)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Part of EPIC #9105 (Computer Use × Vision). This enables and verifies the
**Android on-device screen-understanding loop end-to-end on a Pixel 9a**:
capture the screen with MediaProjection → describe it on the GPU with a local
vision model — no cloud, no ONNX.

## What this does
- **plugin-vision is mobile-loadable**: lazy `sharp` (pure-JS jimp fallback) +
  lazy native YOLO/face detectors, so the module evaluates on Android; it's
  bundled into the mobile agent bundle (literal `import()`), loaded `blocking`
  so its routes register at boot.
- **Agent-triggered screen-capture bridge**: the action requests a frame, the
  renderer (native MediaProjection) captures + submits it back
  (`/api/vision/capture-requests` + `/api/vision/screen-frame`).
- **Native jpeg+scale capture + warm MediaProjection reuse** for battery/latency
  (the renderer asks for `{format:"jpeg",scale:0.5,quality:75}` so resize/encode
  happen natively, and the projection + VirtualDisplay stay warm across frames).
- **Android-14 MediaProjection foreground service**: a `mediaProjection` FGS is
  started before `getMediaProjection()` (API 34 throws `SecurityException`
  otherwise). This was the root blocker for capture.
- **On-device IMAGE_DESCRIPTION**: routed to the bionic GPU host `op:"image"`
  (`eliza-1-2b` + `vision/mmproj-2b.gguf`), with a repetition-collapse so the
  small model's output is bounded/low-token.
- **Crash fix**: the bionic host shared one resident llama context between
  text-generate and vision-describe; prefilling text after a vision (mtmd) eval
  aborted the process (SIGABRT). Now the context is rebuilt only on a
  text↔vision switch, so continuous chat and continuous describe stay warm.

## Verified on Pixel 9a (53081JEBF11586)
- Capture: `captureScreenshot(...)` → `{ok:true,w:540,h:1212,len:15280}`, a real
  540×1212 JPEG of the live home screen; VirtualDisplay
  `ScreenCapture(virtual:ai.elizaos.app)` created with **no SecurityException**.
- Describe: that exact frame → on-device GPU →
  *"The screen shows an orange background with a text input field at the bottom.
  The text input field has a plus icon on the left and a microphone icon on the
  right."* (accurate — the Eliza composer).
- plugin-vision route `GET /api/vision/capture-requests` → 200; renderer bridge
  polling confirmed in logcat.

Evidence: `.github/issue-evidence/9105-android-capture-describe.md` (+ screenshot).

topPRs.title: test(app-control): VIEWS create/edit/delete lifecycle scenario (#9478)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Implements the Tier-1 deliverable of #9478.

## What
Adds `plugins/plugin-app-control/test/scenarios/views-crud-lifecycle.scenario.ts` — the **first scenario that exercises all three owner-authoring sub-modes** of the unified `VIEWS` action (`create`, `edit`, `delete`) in one live-planner run. Existing view scenarios (`views-list`, `views-show`, `views-search`, `views-voice-navigate`) are read/navigate-only.

3 turns → 3 assertions + an honest judge:
- `selectedAction: VIEWS` and `selectedActionArguments includesAny [create|edit|delete|remove]`
- `actionCalled VIEWS minCount: 3`
- a `judgeRubric` requiring the assistant to acknowledge scaffold/dispatch (create), edit dispatch (edit), and confirm-or-not-found (delete), and to **never falsely claim** a view was created, edited, or deleted.

## Why this shape (and what it deliberately does NOT fake)
`VIEWS` create/edit gate on owner access and then **dispatch a coding sub-agent via `START_CODING_TASK`**, which is *not* registered in the scenario runtime (`packages/scenario-runner/src/runtime-factory.ts`) — so a created view never actually registers, and `delete` has no non-protected view to remove. Asserting the **catalog-membership delta** (a view appearing in / disappearing from `GET /api/views`) therefore requires a test-only synthetic-view registration seam in `packages/agent/src/api/views-registry.ts`. That **Tier-2** enhancement is documented inline and in #9478; this PR is the honest routing/dispatch floor it will build on — it does not claim an effect it can't observe.

## Verification
- Modeled field-for-field on the proven `app-create-no-existing` / `app-create-cancel` scenarios in the same directory (same `lane: "live-only"`, `requires`, `finalChecks` shapes).
- Routing dependency confirmed: `VIEWS.validate` for create/edit/delete requires only `ownerCheck` (not view existence) — the **same** `defaultOwnerAccessFn` gate `APP` create uses, whose scenarios already pass in the live lane (`plugins/plugin-app-control/src/actions/views.ts`, `app.ts:145`).
- No coverage-test breakage: `deterministic-action-coverage.test.ts` scans only `packages/scenario-runner/test/scenarios`, not the plugin scenario dir.
```
